items.q: Which component handles visual and spatial information?
items.why: Students mix up the slave systems. The phonological loop is for auditory/verbal information; the sketchpad is for visual/spatial. The central executive coordinates but doesn't store.

items.q: What is the capacity of short-term memory according to Miller?
items.why: Students confuse capacity (items) with duration (about 18-30 seconds without rehearsal). Miller's magic number describes how many CHUNKS, not how long.

items.q: How are phobias MAINTAINED according to Mowrer's two-process model?
items.correct: Through operant conditioning - avoidance reduces anxiety (negative reinforcement).
items.misconception: Through classical conditioning continuing over time.
items.why: Classical conditioning ACQUIRES the phobia; operant conditioning MAINTAINS it. Students conflate these. Avoiding the feared object reduces anxiety, reinforcing the avoidance.

items.q: Which WMM component is described as the 'inner voice'?
items.why: Correct answer: Articulatory process. “Phonological store” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Who added the episodic buffer to the WMM?
items.why: Correct answer: Baddeley (2000). “Atkinson” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: The articulatory loop rehearses items in cycles of approximately how many seconds?
items.why: The real figure is 2. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
